questions.question: What is the average speed of a car moving from point A to point B?

options.B: Average Speed = Total Distance / Total Time

questions.explanation: According to the formula, Average Speed = Total Distance / Total Time (Section 1)

questions.question: What is the unit of speed in the International System of Units (SI)?
questions.explanation: The SI unit of speed is Meter per Second (m/s) as mentioned in the content (Section 3-5)

questions.question: A car travels from city A to city B at an average speed of 60 km/h. How far does it travel in 2 hours?
questions.explanation: Using the formula Distance = Speed * Time, we get Distance = 60 km/h * 2 h = 120 km (Section 1)

questions.question: What is the difference between speed and velocity?

options.A: Speed is a scalar quantity, while velocity is a vector quantity.
options.B: Speed is a vector quantity, while velocity is a scalar quantity.
options.C: Speed and velocity are the same quantities.
options.D: Neither speed nor velocity is a vector or scalar quantity.

questions.explanation: According to the content, Speed is a scalar quantity, while velocity is a vector quantity (Section 2)
